checks.a_analysis: Upon review of the output against the source PDF, no fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. All text present in the output has a basis in the original slides.
The output does not contain any fabricated contact information. While the actual email addresses from the final slide are omitted, no fake ones were inserted.

The conversion pipeline explicitly omitted 11 pages of the source document (Pages 4 through 14) due to a token budget. This results in the complete absence of survey questions Q1 through Q10 and their associated charts and responses.
checks.b_evidence_source: Presentation slides 4 through 14 containing survey results for questions Q1 to Q10.
checks.b_evidence_output: The output includes a JSON object with the note: '_omitted': '11 sections omitted for token budget'.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains charts but no formal data tables. No table defects are present.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are two major text omissions within sections that were otherwise included. First, on Page 2 ('Survey Background'), a primary bullet point and its four sub-bullets describing the survey drafting process are missing. Second, on Page 16 ('Contacts'), all three email addresses provided in the source are missing, leaving only the headings.
checks.b_evidence_source: Slide 2: 'LGC and SGUC drafted 10 survey questions', 'Type of census-related data used', 'Scale of use', 'Decisions based on census-related data', 'Funding based on census-related data'. Slide 16: 'DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us', 'anna.verrill@nc.gov', 'tim.johnson@nc.gov'.
checks.b_evidence_output: Slide 2 output is missing the bullet point regarding drafting and its sub-bullets. Slide 16 output contains headings ('LGC Chair', 'LGC CGIA Staff') but no email content.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Minor text differences are negligible. For instance, the output correctly captures the slight grammatical error in the source ('Total of 52 response collected') rather than correcting it to plural, which shows faithful transcription where content was captured.

checks.a_analysis: I scanned the JSON output for any repeated text blocks or duplicated elements. The NC DIT logo appears on multiple pages (elements 5, 8, 30, 242, 272), but this is consistent with its appearance as a slide footer/logo on every page of the source PDF and is explicitly listed as an acceptable repetition.

The heading hierarchy is logical and follows a standard pattern. Page 1 uses H1 for title information. Major slide titles (e.g., 'Survey Background', 'Contacts') use H1. Sub-sections within 'Contacts' use H2. No levels are skipped (e.g., H1 to H3).
The source document contains no tables; the data is presented via lists and charts. Therefore, no table structures were corrupted.

The reading order follows the flow of the presentation slides correctly. Information is presented from title page through to contact information in the intended sequence. While some content is missing (noted in S5/S7), the relative order of the remaining content is accurate.

On Page 2, the list conversion is incomplete. The source document contains a primary bullet point ('LGC and SGUC drafted 10 survey questions') with a nested unordered list of four items. The JSON output omits this entire section of the list, resulting in a flat list of only three items.
checks.b_evidence_source:
• LGC and SGUC drafted 10 survey questions
    • Type of census-related data used
    • Scale of use
    • Decisions based on census-related data
    • Funding based on census-related data
checks.b_evidence_output: The entire parent item and nested sub-list are missing from the items array in element index 7.

checks.a_analysis: The contact information on Page 16 contains three email addresses (DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us, anna.verrill@nc.gov, tim.johnson@nc.gov). These are clearly visible in the source PDF as contact links/text but are completely missing as text or functional links in the output, appearing only as empty-alt-text image captures (elements 273-278).
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content and links for these emails are missing from the elements list under the 'Contacts' section.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML conversion follows the correct sequence of information from the source PDF slide deck. However, the visual layout is severely marred by recurring, massive black rectangular blocks that appear after every section corresponding to a slide. These blocks are roughly the same size as the content blocks themselves, disrupting the visual flow and organization to the point that the document feels broken or corrupted compared to the original slide presentation. While the transition from fixed slides to a single-column responsive layout is expected and acceptable, these disruptive artifacts constitute a major visual layout failure.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF maintains a consistent and clean visual flow across 16 slides, with text and charts placed on white or dark blue backgrounds.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows large, full-width black rectangular blocks following almost every content section (e.g., after 'Survey Background', 'Survey Solicitation', and 'Q1').
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, appearing after each section corresponding to slides 2 through 16.

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no formal tables. Lists of information (such as on Page 11) are correctly rendered as text lists in the HTML.

While the data visualizations (charts for Q1, Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7) are rendered correctly, the branding imagery is broken. The 'NC DIT' logo, which appears on every slide in the source PDF, is only correctly rendered once at the top of the HTML. Subsequent instances appear to have failed, resulting in the large black rectangular blocks that populate the document. Instead of being correctly displayed or properly suppressed for accessibility (if considered decorative), these images are rendered as broken visual artifacts.
checks.b_evidence_source: NC DIT logo branding appearing at the bottom right of pages 2-16 in the source PDF.
checks.b_evidence_output: Large black rectangular blocks appearing in the HTML screenshot where the logos or slide-end branding should be.
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout the document, at the end of each content section.

checks.a_analysis: The document includes a table of contents at the beginning which links to headings in the body. This is an intentional accessibility feature and not a duplication defect. No actual body content (paragraphs, tables, or charts) is duplicated in the rendering.
